db7f57fa-cb06-43e8-8103-feb7fd882984.name: Ember Vault of the Five Rings

children.text: Ember Vault of the Five Rings
Legendary Booty of the Imperial Houses

This hoard is said to have been amassed by a coalition of Imperial clans during the Siege of Kharis. Stored for centuries beneath the courts of Rokugan, it awaits those bold enough to claim its honor-bound treasures.
A gemstone seal inscribed with a House symbol
Fine silk and lacquer design, decorative
Embroidered with the symbol of the Phoenix Clan
Spell Scroll (Protection from Evil and Good)
Scroll of Protection from Evil and Good spell
Required: Requires attunement by a creature with Strength or Dexterity 13 or higher.
This jade-hued polearm features a curved blade of pale green stone mounted on a lacquered haft. Delicate dragon motifs coil around the shaft, their eyes set with tiny emeralds.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Once per long rest, as an action, you can exhale a shockwave of dragon kin: all creatures in a 15-foot cone must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Forged in the volcanic forges beneath Mount Ryoshima, legends say this weapon was wielded by the dragon lord Areku the Unbound.
This delicate fan is crafted from reinforced lacquered wood, its steel ribs honed to a fine edge. Painted cranes take flight across its surface.

When you hit with a thrown attack using this fan, you can choose to distract the target. The target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to 3 + your Dexterity modifier (minimum 1). All expended uses recharge after a long rest.
Once wielded by Lady Mirai of the Crane Clan, famed for her graceful yet deadly battlefield maneuvers.
Required: Requires attunement by a creature of non-evil alignment.
This lacquered mask depicts a snarling oni, its eye sockets glowing faintly red. Crimson ribbons secure it around the wearer's head.

While wearing the mask, you gain advantage on Intimidation checks. Once per short rest, you can use an action to unleash the Oni's Bellow. Each creature in a 20-foot cone must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or be deafened and take 2d8 thunder damage, or half damage on a successful save.
Forged by an exiled Kitsuki artisan, it was said to house the trapped roar of an oni slain in the Shadowlands.
